steps.title: Test all components on the bench
steps.description: Before assembly, connect each component individually to verify it works: spin motors, check FC connects to Betaflight, verify camera outputs video, bind receiver.
tips: Use smoke stopper for first power-on
Save time debugging later

steps.title: Prepare the frame
steps.description: Remove protective film from carbon plates. Check for cracks. Install standoffs on the bottom plate.
tips: Check for hairline cracks
Use blue Loctite on standoff screws

steps.title: Mount ESC and solder power
steps.description: Solder XT60 pigtail to ESC power pads. Add a 1000μF low-ESR capacitor across battery pads. Mount ESC to frame bottom plate using rubber standoffs.
tips: Tin pads and wires before joining
Capacitor as close to battery pads as possible
steps.warning: Double check polarity on XT60 — reversed polarity will destroy the ESC instantly

steps.title: Mount FC on ESC stack
steps.description: Connect FC to ESC via ribbon cable or solder signal wires. Use rubber grommets between FC and standoffs for vibration isolation.
tips: Arrow on FC must point forward
Don't over-tighten stack bolts

steps.title: Solder receiver to FC
steps.description: Solder receiver TX/RX/5V/GND wires to the correct UART pads on the FC. Mount receiver with double-sided tape, antenna out through the frame.
tips: TX goes to RX pad and vice versa
Route antenna away from VTX and ESC

steps.title: Install motors
steps.description: Secure motors to frame arms using appropriate screws. Solder motor wires to ESC motor pads (order doesn't matter — you'll fix direction in software).
tips: Don't use screws too long — they'll hit motor windings
Keep motor wires same length
steps.warning: Screws that are too long will short motor windings and destroy the motor

steps.title: Configure Betaflight
steps.description: Connect FC to computer. Set up: motor protocol (DSHOT600), receiver protocol (CRSF), verify motor direction, calibrate accelerometer, set up modes (Arm, Beeper, Angle).
tips: Back up your config with CLI 'dump'
Test motor direction with props OFF

steps.title: Install camera and VTX
steps.description: Mount camera in frame cage. Mount VTX with double-sided tape. Solder camera/VTX video and power wires to FC. Connect antenna to VTX.
tips: Never power VTX without antenna — it will burn out
Route video wires away from motor wires

steps.title: Final assembly and first flight
steps.description: Cable management with zip ties. Mount props (correct direction!). Smoke test with smoke stopper. Then go fly!
tips: Double check prop direction matches motor spin
First hover at 1 foot for 10 seconds to verify stability

steps.title: Bench test all components
steps.description: Verify each component works individually before building.
tips: Use smoke stopper

steps.title: Assemble frame
steps.description: Build the Apex frame. Install standoffs. Check arm fit.
tips: Loctite on all frame bolts

steps.title: Solder ESC power and capacitor
steps.description: XT60 to ESC. Add 35V 1000μF cap.
tips: Use 35V cap for 6S — 25V will pop
steps.warning: 6S voltage can exceed 25V — use 35V rated capacitor minimum

steps.title: Install FC stack
steps.description: Mount ESC, then FC. Solder motor and receiver wires.
tips: Double check UART mapping

steps.title: Motor installation and soldering
steps.description: Mount motors. Solder 3 wires per motor to ESC pads.
tips: Check screw length for motor size

steps.title: Betaflight configuration
steps.description: DSHOT600, bidirectional DSHOT enabled, RPM filter ON. CRSF receiver. PID defaults. Set up GPS rescue if GPS installed.
tips: Enable RPM filter — huge improvement on 6S

steps.title: Camera, VTX, and final wiring
steps.description: Mount camera and VTX. Solder video path. Configure SmartAudio. OSD setup.
tips: Never power VTX without antenna

steps.title: Maiden flight
steps.description: Smoke test. Mount props. Hover test. Fly and enjoy the 6S power!
tips: 6S is punchy — start with low rates

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.description: Assemble the toothpick frame. These are simple — usually just a top and bottom plate.
tips: Toothpick frames are fragile — handle gently

steps.title: Mount AIO flight controller
steps.description: AIO boards have ESC built in. Mount with rubber grommets on 20x20 pattern.
tips: AIO simplifies wiring significantly

steps.title: Solder motors
steps.description: 4 motor wires each. Toothpick motors are small — use fine solder tip.
tips: Use thin solder wire for small pads

steps.title: Camera and VTX setup
steps.description: Camera and VTX are usually combined on these builds. Wire and mount.
tips: Cable manage carefully — space is tight

steps.title: Configure and fly
steps.description: Flash Betaflight, set up receiver, verify motors, and go fly indoor or outdoor.
tips: Great for park flying and indoors

steps.title: Test everything on bench
steps.description: Verify all components including GPS module getting satellite fix.
tips: GPS needs outdoor test — won't fix indoors

steps.title: Frame assembly
steps.description: 7-inch frames are larger. Take time with standoffs and motor mounts.
tips: Longer arms need Loctite on all screws

steps.title: ESC and power wiring
steps.description: Solder ESC power. Add 35V capacitor. 7-inch draws more current — clean solder joints critical.
tips: Use thicker wire for battery leads (14AWG)

steps.title: FC stack and GPS wiring
steps.description: Mount FC. Wire GPS to UART. Wire Crossfire RX to another UART.
tips: GPS TX→FC RX and GPS RX→FC TX

steps.title: Motor installation
steps.description: Mount 2806.5 motors on 7-inch arms. Solder motor wires.
tips: Verify motor mounting pattern matches frame

steps.title: Betaflight setup with GPS Rescue
steps.description: Configure GPS, GPS Rescue, failsafe, Crossfire CRSF. Set low rates for smooth flying.
tips: Set GPS Rescue altitude to 50m
Test failsafe on bench
steps.warning: Always configure and test failsafe before long range flights

steps.title: Camera, VTX, final assembly
steps.description: Mount camera and VTX. GPS module on top of frame on elevated mount for clear sky view.
tips: GPS antenna must face skyward, away from carbon fiber

steps.title: Range test and maiden
steps.description: Test radio range at ground level. Verify GPS fix (10+ sats). Then maiden flight.
tips: Start with short flights to verify GPS rescue works
